perturbation.original_text: Licensor and Licensee will be entitled to terminate this Agreement by written notice to the other party in the event the other party is in material breach of any of its obligations hereunder and shall fail to remedy any such default within one hundred twenty (120) days after notice thereof by the non-breaching party.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor and Licensee will be entitled to terminate this Agreement by written notice to the other party in the event the other party is in material breach of any of its obligations hereunder and shall fail to remedy any such default within thirty (30) days after notice thereof by the non-breaching party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the cure period for a material breach from 120 days to 30 days. This directly conflicts with Section 1.5, which specifies a 120-day period for restoring the quality of Licensed Products after notification by Licensor. The change creates uncertainty about which cure period applies in the event of a quality-related breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the quality of a class of the Licensed Products falls below such standards, Licensee shall use commercially reasonable efforts to restore such quality. In the event that Licensee has not taken appropriate steps to restore such quality within one-hundred twenty (120) days after notification by Licensor, Licensor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the cure period for a material breach from 120 days to 30 days. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which provides a 120-day cure period for restoring the quality of licensed products. This creates a clear inconsistency and uncertainty regarding the applicable cure period for quality-related breaches, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by Licensee without the consent of Licensor which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such consent of Licensor is required under this Agreement in the event of a Change of Control of Licensee so long as: (a) the resulting, surviving or transferee Person assumes all the obligations of the Licensee by operation of Law or pursuant to an agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Licensor; and (b) the licenses granted herein shall not be transferrable or sublicensable to Affiliates of such Person unless such Affiliates were Affiliates of Licensee prior to such Change of Control.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by Licensee without the consent of Licensor which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, such consent of Licensor is explicitly required under this Agreement in the event of a Change of Control of Licensee so long as: (a) the resulting, surviving or transferee Person assumes all the obligations of the Licensee by operation of Law or pursuant to an agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Licensor; and (b) the licenses granted herein shall not be transferrable or sublicensable to Affiliates of such Person unless such Affiliates were Affiliates of Licensee prior to such Change of Control.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Originally, no consent was required in case of change of control if certain conditions were met. The modified clause now states that consent *is* required, even if those same conditions are met, creating an in-text contradiction regarding whether Licensor consent is needed for assignment under change of control circumstances, creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by Licensee without the consent of Licensor which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the foregoing, such consent of Licensor is explicitly required under this Agreement in the event of a Change of Control of Licensee so long as: (a) the resulting, surviving or transferee Person assumes all the obligations of the Licensee by operation of Law or pursuant to an agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Licensor; and (b) the licenses granted herein shall not be transferrable or sublicensable to Affiliates of such Person unless such Affiliates were Affiliates of Licensee prior to such Change of Control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the original intent. The original text stated that consent was *not* required for assignment in the event of a change of control under specific circumstances. The changed text states that consent *is* explicitly required under the same circumstances. The contradiction occurs because the change alters 'no consent is required' to 'consent is explicitly required', triggering an in-text contradiction and legal uncertainty about Licensor consent during a change of control.

perturbation.original_text: All print and electronic displays of the Licensed Mark by Licensee shall include at Licensor's option, a notice to the effect that the Licensed Mark are owned by Licensor and used by Licensee under license from Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: All print and electronic displays of the Licensed Mark by Licensee shall include a notice to the effect that the Licensed Mark are owned by Licensor and used by Licensee under license from Licensor. This is mandatory and cannot be waived by the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the Licensor's option to decide whether the trademark notice is included on displays. This introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 1.5, which states that, 'To the extent reasonably practicable, all Licensed Products shall include notices on labeling and packaging for the Licensed Products stating that the Licensed Mark is owned by Licensor and used by Licensee under license from Licensor.'. The 'reasonably practicable' clause implies some flexibility, whereas the modified Section 1.3 makes the notice mandatory without exception, which creates ambiguity over the level of discretion the Licensee has.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To the extent reasonably practicable, all Licensed Products shall include notices on labeling and packaging for the Licensed Products stating that the Licensed Mark is owned by Licensor and used by Licensee under license from Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 1.3 allowed the Licensor the option of requiring a trademark notice, whereas the changed text makes this notice mandatory. This directly contradicts Section 1.5, which states that notices on Licensed Products are required 'to the extent reasonably practicable.' The changed Section 1.3 removes any flexibility, while Section 1.5 allows for it, creating a strong and direct conflict regarding the obligation to include trademark notices.
